days.summary: A high-volume day focused on stabilizing Agent Team workflows, expanding the slash menu and plus-button command experience, refining integrations and settings surfaces, and strengthening E2E coverage. Backend agent-core work added Agent Architect and AI Researcher skill sources, workspace resource loading controls, external import scope handling, and event store refactors, while frontend changes improved skill pills, MCP/skills integrations, source control, workspace setup, CodeMirror behavior, table interactions, loading states, and localized UI copy.
frontendChangeBullets: Expanded the plus-button command menu with dedicated Mode and Models flyouts, making common session setup choices easier to discover without leaving the composer.
Improved skill pills across Agent Team and chat surfaces so selected skills are more visible, safer to expand, and clearer during active workflow setup.
Refined MCP and skills integration screens with more stable setup flows, clearer loading behavior, and stronger feedback when integration data is unavailable.
Polished source control and workspace setup interactions so users get smoother file, repository, and project-context handling inside ORGII.
Improved CodeMirror behavior, table interactions, loading states, and localized interface copy for a more consistent product experience across settings and workflow screens.
backendChangeBullets: Added workspace resource loading controls so agents can more deliberately manage which project resources are available as runtime context.
Added Agent Architect and AI Researcher skill sources in agent-core, expanding the set of structured capabilities available to agent workflows.
Improved external import scope handling so imported rules, skills, and agent definitions can be applied more predictably to the intended workspace context.
Refactored event store internals to improve reliability around persisted agent activity and runtime event handling.
Expanded E2E coverage around Agent Team workflows, slash menu behavior, integrations, and settings surfaces to catch regressions in both UI and runtime paths.

days.summary: ORGII work added the Canvas inline rendering path, canvas preview WorkStation tabs, Git attribution controls, richer pinned action and model pill behavior, queue editing, Ops Control list view, Git remote fixes, and broader chat/input stability work. The day also refined slash menu flyout navigation, Open Browser wiring into WorkStation, hover-state fixes, and E2E coverage for slash mode and plan lifecycle behavior.
frontendChangeBullets: Added Canvas support across chat and WorkStation, including inline canvas blocks in conversations and a dedicated canvas preview tab for richer visual artifacts.
Improved pinned actions and model pill behavior in the chat composer so active actions, selected models, and refresh states are easier to understand.
Added queue editing support to chat input workflows, giving users more control over pending messages and follow-up work before execution.
Introduced an Ops Control list view so operational tasks can be browsed and managed in a denser, more scannable format.
Improved slash menu flyout navigation, Open Browser wiring into WorkStation, hover states, and input-area stability for smoother keyboard and pointer workflows.
backendChangeBullets: Added the Canvas inline rendering path so canvas artifacts can flow from agent output into chat and WorkStation runtime surfaces.
Improved Git attribution controls and remote-helper behavior, strengthening repository operations used by source control and WorkStation flows.
Expanded E2E coverage for slash mode behavior, plan lifecycle handling, and canvas-related interactions to protect complex session flows.
Improved queue and pinned-action handling behind the chat input so pending work can be represented more safely in session state.
Refined operational task data handling for Ops Control so runtime task lists can support the new list-view experience.

days.summary: Work centered on decoupling Work Items from project scope, introducing the Changelog timeline view, polishing chat composer and plus-menu interactions, and adding the ORGII high contrast theme. The day also expanded KeyVault, Git preferences, Agent settings, and agent-core test coverage while continuing cleanup around markdown editing, terminal agents, tutorial tours, shortcuts, and WorkStation sidebar chrome.
frontendChangeBullets: Added the Changelog timeline view so users can browse product progress directly inside MainApp.
Decoupled Work Items from project scope, making standalone work easier to create and manage outside a single project container.
Improved chat composer interactions, including plus-menu focus restoration, keyboard navigation, display-mode pills, skill pill previews, and safer pill text rendering in user bubbles.
Added the ORGII high contrast theme and refined sidebar hover animations, collapsed WorkStation chrome, shortcut tooltips, and sidebar toggle behavior.
Replaced remaining Tiptap editing surfaces with the shared markdown editor and polished dropdown, context-menu, and project dropdown interactions.
backendChangeBullets: Expanded agent-core and agent DTO coverage with extended tests around definitions, provider wiring, and agent configuration behavior.
Aligned Rust test inputs with the display_text field used by chat pill rendering, improving consistency between persisted messages and UI display data.
Added Terminal Agent support and updated terminal assets used by the embedded terminal workflow.
Stabilized routine reruns, settings hit testing, webpack dev startup, and DOM event target handling to reduce regressions in daily development flows.
Expanded E2E coverage for KeyVault settings, Git preferences, Agent settings, rendered agent selector paths, and Work Item flows.

days.summary: A WorkStation and chat-panel heavy day refined project and work-item creation inside chat, added PR creation and lookup surfaces to Source Control, improved GitHub authentication feedback, and consolidated diff navigation. Canvas simulator work added setup_repo routing, simulator app views, and chat-to-simulator jumps, while broader refactors improved app chrome, tab drag-and-drop utilities, markdown rendering performance, and session launch coverage.
frontendChangeBullets: Moved project and work-item creation deeper into the chat panel so users can start project work and agent sessions from the conversation surface.
Added PR creation and open-PR quick-action UI in WorkStation Source Control, including clearer section layout, filter controls, and PR pill behavior.
Improved GitHub auth failure handling by routing users to Settings and showing clearer feedback when PR creation cannot authenticate.
Added canvas simulator app surfaces, setup_repo chat blocks, and chat-to-simulator jumps for richer generated-app previews.
Refined app chrome, inline work-item properties, sidebar hover behavior, tab drag-and-drop reuse, and markdown rendering performance.
backendChangeBullets: Added the github_find_pull_request Tauri command and PR state wiring used by WorkStation Source Control and shared PR creation flows.
Improved Source Control status refresh behavior and local GitHub token fallback handling for PR workflows.
Added Rust support for canvas simulator app routing and setup_repo blocks so chat output can open richer simulator experiences.
Expanded rendered E2E coverage for provider matrix wiring, Ask mode, custom agent launch, session launch wiring, Work Items, and agent selector flows.
Consolidated diff file navigation and layout settings behind WorkStation sidebar patterns for more maintainable source-control behavior.

days.summary: The busiest day of the range focused on WorkStation PR v2, queued session controls, GitHub device-flow authentication, source-control filtering, and diff-to-chat workflows. It also hardened session persistence by serializing SQLite writers, cleaned up native session terminal state, removed the built-in apply_patch tool, renamed the Tauri binary to orgii, and swept TypeScript, lint, audit, and circular-dependency issues across the app.
frontendChangeBullets: Redesigned the WorkStation PR section in the Source Control sidebar with shared tokens, compact actions, clearer eligibility rules, and safer recreate behavior after closed or merged PRs.
Added Pull Request filtering in Source Control and wired PR lookup into the CodeEditor-level workflow so PR context follows the active file and branch.
Improved queued session controls, cache saves, and UI behavior after rebase so pending actions remain stable during complex chat flows.
Added diff selection actions that copy file-reference clipboard data and send selected changes into chat, making review and follow-up prompts faster.

Polished chat, composer, and WorkStation surfaces with global chat collapse controls, workstation context pills, file-change diff pills, improved composer placement, and steadier tab and scroll state.
backendChangeBullets: Serialized SQLite writers to prevent sessions.db lock storms during high-volume native session activity.
Finalized native session terminal state more cleanly so Rust-backed sessions shut down and report terminal status more reliably.
Added Rust GitHub PR commands and consolidated GitHub device-flow authentication around a baked public client id and local token fallback behavior.
Renamed the Tauri main binary from app to orgii and updated related command and CI expectations.
Removed the built-in apply_patch tool, switched skill installs to skills.sh snapshots, enforced Conventional Commits with commitlint, and swept outstanding TypeScript and lint errors.

days.summary: Work focused on benchmark batch workflows, push-to-talk voice input, GitHub Issues integration, chat queue hardening, replay search rendering, and compacted Rust session history. The day also improved coordinator responsiveness, session diff routing, multi-root composer behavior, browser inspect cleanup, sidebar keybindings, and Source Control-aligned issue surfaces.
frontendChangeBullets: Added push-to-talk voice input in chat, expanding composer interaction options for hands-free or quick spoken prompts.
Added benchmark batch session and reusable batch task management flows, with clearer benchmark session headers and skipped-evaluation failure reporting.
Introduced GitHub Issues UI in WorkStation with sidebar wiring, design-system components, icon buttons, and Source Control-aligned filter behavior.
Improved replay search result rendering, sticky notes board behavior, inline ghost fields, status bar spacing, and pinned-page back navigation.
Hardened chat queue controls, Stop semantics, session controls, file mentions, multi-root composer edge cases, and isolated UI E2E coverage.
backendChangeBullets: Added GitHub Issues backend commands, atoms, helpers, and localized strings to support issue browsing from WorkStation surfaces.
Persisted compacted Rust session history and verified compacted provider payloads so long-running agent sessions retain cleaner historical state.
Kept Agent Team coordinator sessions responsive and coordinator roots visible during active multi-agent workflows.
Avoided global CLI resume lock stalls, improving reliability when multiple CLI-backed sessions resume or continue in parallel.
Updated the fast-open package name, webdriver app target, GitHub adapter wording, and pre-commit cleanup paths used by the June workflow.

days.summary: June 9 brought a major Source Control and PR consolidation push alongside Canvas, workspace launch, queue, and chat polish. Work added a list-first PR sidebar, shared PR status helpers, source-control session filtering, external agent history import, GUI context guide highlights, React 19 migration work, and broader cleanup around PR operations, queue lifecycle, theme switching, A2UI canvas imports, and launchpad actions.
frontendChangeBullets: Redesigned PR browsing around a list-only sidebar with details in the header bar, compact rows, PR status strings, filters, collapse controls, and safer commit selection behavior.
Added source-control session filtering and refined CodeMirror diff behavior so users can navigate file changes and session-linked diffs more reliably.
Added external agent history import plus launchpad action and clone-progress improvements for smoother workspace onboarding.

Improved chat surfaces with removable image attachments while editing, pinned-action controls, terminal clone flow, streaming placeholders, title-only list_dir output, and longer message preview thresholds.
Expanded Canvas rendering with A2UI loading skeletons, streaming indicators, and table/chart/form documentation while resolving package import and webpack alias issues.
backendChangeBullets: Consolidated PR status formatting, shared PR helpers, and WorkStation PR wiring so duplicate operations and dead API paths were removed.
Unified queue turn lifecycle through the FSM and dispatcher, including safer natural-drain behavior and transient-session todo stability.
Added external agent history import plumbing and updated ADE Manager scope and security defaults for agent configuration cleanup.
Migrated the frontend baseline to React 19 and cleared lint, TypeScript, circular-dependency, webpack alias, and A2UI import regressions.
Synchronized architecture-audit and E2E skill documentation while trimming multi-repo acceptance noise for the new testing baseline.

days.summary: A heavy UI-stabilization day focused on DOM preview, diff and replay rendering, portal/zoom correctness, WorkStation tabs, changelog wiki publishing, and chat-composer behavior. The day also added loading shimmers across more surfaces, a WorkStation DOM component preview tab, plan-approved communication cards, GitHub-backed updater docs, session replay fixes, KeyVault OAuth refresh unification, and broad E2E repairs.
frontendChangeBullets: Added DOM component preview support in WorkStation, including JSON paste pills, iframe loading behavior, external stylesheet support, stricter selector matching, and natural-size rendering.

Polished chat and composer UX with editable image removal, git commit reference cards, terminal preview expand controls, preserved mode-switch text, session title hover cards, and more reliable pill deletion and draft persistence.
Improved diff and replay views with flat FileHeader styling, real line numbers for ranged reads, multi-hunk line-number fixes, session git submission display, and scrollable wide markdown tables.
Fixed portal, menu, webview, and tooltip positioning under CSS zoom, including native webview coordinate scaling and zoom-corrected viewport sizing.
Published Changelog wiki pages in English, French, and Chinese, added the design philosophy document, wired more loading shimmers, and simplified WorkStation tab chrome.
backendChangeBullets: Switched releases and updater delivery toward GitHub and excluded the wiki from the main repo, aligning changelog publishing with the release flow.
Unified KeyVault model refresh across OAuth providers and simplified integration channel setup for less duplicated provider logic.
Removed database simulator tools and the sticky notes feature while renaming shells to windows and moving app control into Spotlight.
Repaired CLI rewind, E2E open-session retries, plan-update prompts, and file-change panel waits to bring live UI coverage back into shape.
Cleared circular dependencies, prettier issues, i18n gaps across all 13 locales, and temporary regressions from diff, queue, and session-replay work.

days.summary: June 11 focused on release and runtime architecture: platform-specific Tauri config, CI and signing improvements, sidecar notarization, Rust modularization, turn-intent lifecycle storage, ADE Manager integration, Source Control repo awareness, and Simulator visibility. Frontend work refined page/sidebar transparency, DOM pills, chat pills, navigation context pills, background subagent pinning, source-control issues and PR panels, and session sharing.
frontendChangeBullets: Added page and sidebar transparency across chrome layers while refining layout, sharing/export dialogs, modal footers, selected-row styling, and session UI surfaces.
Improved WorkStation Source Control, Issues, PR panels, session repo hints, sidebar search filtering, and My Station behavior so the active session's repo is easier to follow.
Added drag-to-composer context pills from navigation items, DOM selected-element JSON pills from the browser, and stronger unbounded-content caps for PR, terminal, paste, and DOM pills.
Pinned background subagent workers above the composer and tightened chat-pill, session-card, planning-footer, and replay rendering behavior across long-running sessions.
Added persistent database query history, editor line-number navigation, loading and preview polish, and live-session UX fixes for long turns.
backendChangeBullets: Split Tauri configuration into platform-specific overrides and improved Windows, macOS signing, notarization, sidecar, and updater release workflows.
Threaded turn_intent_id and TurnIntentBridgeSource through frontend dispatch, backend scheduling, lifecycle storage, and send_message_impl so turns have clearer runtime identity.
Modularized large Rust areas including dev-record IDE history, inbox drain, LSP servers, VSC database models, sync/io, and tool call context handling.
Made Rust the single truth for simulator visibility and workspace path authorization while isolating IDE sync and live backend echo paths.
Removed dead code, mobile remote control, coding_agent, unused marketplace SDK package, redundant CI workflow, and leftover rate-limit tracker paths.

days.summary: June 12 combined build/release acceleration, session sharing, native app reliability, source-control improvements, expanded unit and E2E coverage, and agent runtime hardening. Work added WebRTC session sharing, Commit & Push quick actions, RAM monitor breakdowns, faster parallel builds, PATH propagation fixes, subagent monitor replay improvements, store/hook/engine tests, CI and security documentation, and stronger cancellation and transcript handling.
frontendChangeBullets: Added WebRTC session sharing with polished sharing and export dialogs, restored remote shared-session dispatch, and improved session UI modal behavior.
Added a Commit & Push quick-action pill and improved source-control, issue callback, PR panel, file-manager reveal, diff-review, and editor navigation flows.
Improved simulator and replay surfaces with live-tailed subagent cells, terminal-state clip handling, hidden simulator replay navigation icons, todo-block reuse, and null-argument handling.

Polished chat, station, and UI chrome with pinned todo backdrops, compact WebDevTools placeholders, copy feedback localization, pagination icons, diff preview truncation, and safer local-path handling.

Added store, hook, engine, keyboard, model, ActionSystem, util, and rendered E2E coverage across tabs, Git diffs, session dispatch, ChatPanel blocks, Simulator, SessionCore, and subagent monitor clips.
backendChangeBullets: Made fast builds parallel by default with esbuild production bundling, lld, more Rust jobs, skipped local codesign, and faster release-build paths.
Fixed native app startup, window, PATH, terminal, traffic-light, and sidecar spawn behavior across macOS and Windows packaging flows.
Improved agent runtime robustness with immutable transcripts, sequence-based truncation, Anthropic max_tokens handling, subagent context-explosion safeguards, and DeepSeek model id formatting.
Expanded CI, security, architecture, TEST_CASES, and skill-routing documentation while adding Windows x64 build, Trusted Signing, and notarized sidecar download strategy work.
Removed stale toast shims, renamed navigation sidebar atoms, extracted shared dropdown utilities, and cleaned dead diagnostics, aliases, swagger UI, and GitHub issue pagination paths.
